CREATE TABLE test_strings (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, str_value VARCHAR(255));
SELECT ltrim('abcabcabc', 'abc') AS trimmed_string FROM (SELECT str_value FROM test_strings WHERE str_value LIKE 'a%') AS temp;
SELECT ltrim(CONCAT(str_value, 'aaaa'), 'a') FROM test_strings WHERE id < 4;
SELECT * FROM (SELECT id, ltrim(str_value, 'a') AS trimmed FROM test_strings WHERE str_value LIKE 'a%') AS subquery WHERE trimmed <> '';
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_strings WHERE ltrim(str_value, 'a') = '';
CREATE TABLE second_table (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, ref_id INTEGER, description VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO second_table (ref_id, description) VALUES (1, 'First Description'), (2, 'Second Description'), (3, 'Third Description');
SELECT st.id, ltrim(ts.str_value, 'a') AS trimmed_value FROM test_strings ts JOIN second_table st ON ts.id = st.ref_id ORDER BY st.id;
SELECT id, ltrim(str_value, 'abc') AS trimmed_value FROM test_strings WHERE id IN (SELECT ref_id FROM second_table WHERE description LIKE 'First%');
SELECT ltrim('      whitespace      ', ' ') AS trimmed FROM test_strings;
